name: WESTWATER RESOURCES, INC.
business: Westwater Resources is developing battery-grade natural graphite materials, primarily CSPG for lithium-ion batteries, and expects to produce ULTRA-CSPG and SG Fines at its Kellyton Graphite Plant. The company holds mineral rights for the Coosa Graphite Deposit to supply future feedstock. It is targeting domestic and allied markets to reduce reliance on imports.

exposures.sentence: CSPG is primarily used in anode active material (AAM) in lithium-ion batteries.

Our target markets for our products include electric vehicles, trucks and buses, consumer electronics, defense and aerospace applications, and battery energy storage system (BESS) batteries for grid and data center applications.

Our target markets for our products include electric vehicles, trucks and buses, consumer electronics, defense and aerospace applications, and battery energy storage system (BESS) batteries for grid and data center applications.

Our target markets for our products include electric vehicles, trucks and buses, consumer electronics, defense and aerospace applications, and battery energy storage system (BESS) batteries for grid and data center applications.

Our target markets for our products include electric vehicles, trucks and buses, consumer electronics, defense and aerospace applications, and battery energy storage system (BESS) batteries for grid and data center applications.

Our target markets for our products include electric vehicles, trucks and buses, consumer electronics, defense and aerospace applications, and battery energy storage system (BESS) batteries for grid and data center applications.
In addition to graphite, the Coosa Graphite Deposit contains vanadium, which the Company plans to explore and evaluate for potential technical feasibility of extraction and processing in the future.

catalysts.event: Received U.S. patent for graphite purification method
catalysts.sentence: On September 17, 2025, the Company announced it had received its U.S. Patent related to its graphite purification method.

catalysts.event: Announced plans to progress permitting for Coosa Graphite Deposit
catalysts.sentence: On October 27, 2025, Westwater announced plans to progress the permitting process for future mine development at its Coosa Graphite Deposit.

catalysts.event: Filed NPDES permit application and FAST-41 submission
catalysts.sentence: Subsequent to year end, the Company filed an application for a National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permit with the Alabama Department of Environmental Management (ADEM) and submitted its project application to the FAST-41 Federal Permitting Council dashboard.

catalysts.event: Received letter of interest from EXIM for Kellyton Graphite Plant
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, Westwater received a letter of interest from EXIM related to the Kellyton Graphite Plant, under EXIM’s Make More in America Initiative and China and Transformational Exports Program.
On November 3, 2025, FCA terminated its Offtake Agreement with the Company.

tamClaims.claim: Global lithium-ion battery demand surpassed 1 TWh in 2024 and is expected to increase substantially.
tamClaims.sentence: Battery demand surpassed 1 TWh in 2024 and is expected to increase substantially over the coming decade.

tamClaims.claim: China accounted for approximately 76% of global flake graphite output in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: China accounted for approximately 76% of global flake graphite output, supported by a low-cost production base.

tamClaims.claim: China produces around 90% of graphite anodes globally.
tamClaims.sentence: China currently produces around 90% of graphite anodes globally.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. LFP battery pipeline increased from 178.9 GWh to 287.7 GWh between January and November 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: Benchmark’s assessed U.S. LFP pipeline increased from 178.9 GWh to 287.7 GWh between January and November 2025.
